peer_exemplars.reasoning: High openness to ideas and dutifulness support transparency in logic, but very low anti-elitism and high authoritarian submission temper enthusiasm for government-mandated disclosure.

Her extreme libertarian anti-censorship orientation (98.59) and high anti-domination moral foundation (96.97) drive strong support for making platform algorithms transparent, but moderate trust in science (57.81) tempers the score slightly.

High openness and very high extraversion, combined with low agreeableness and strong anti-domination values, drive support for transparency as a check on opaque power, but moderate trust in institutions tempers it to a clear but not maximal stance.
High authoritarianism and low openness to experience lead to skepticism of algorithmic transparency as it may undermine social control.

High trust in institutions and very high agreeableness combine with strong anti-censorship and libertarian skepticism of state control to produce clear support for mandated algorithmic transparency as a check on opaque corporate power, though not maximal because of residual deference to legitimate authority.

Very low openness and high authoritarian submission make me skeptical of mandated transparency that could disrupt established platform operations, though my high agreeableness tempers outright opposition.
High trust in institutions and science supports transparency, but low digital literacy and libertarian anti-censorship orientation temper enthusiasm.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Opposition arises from very low trust in government and judiciary, combined with high right-wing authoritarian submission that makes official disinformation agencies feel like coercive state control.

Strong anti-authoritarian and anti-domination values, combined with very low authoritarian submission and low system justification, produce mild opposition to a centralized agency that could be co-opted by elites, despite high trust in government.

Very high system justification but also high libertarian anti-censorship and strong skepticism of coercive social control create internal conflict, leading to mild opposition due to fear a dedicated agency would become an authoritarian tool, overriding the high trust in government.

Extremely high authoritarian aggression (90.07) and conventionalism (99.66) combined with very low anti-elitism (16.32) make me deeply suspicious of a government agency that could be used to suppress traditional or nationalist viewpoints.
High trust in government and experts supports such agencies, but libertarian skepticism of state control and low authoritarianism moderate support.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Mild distrust reflecting very low trust in government (31.18) and judiciary (9.31) combined with high system justification (59.82) and dutifulness that partially curbs this skepticism.

Strong opposition to state control (94.11) and very low system justification (1.77 political) actually drive distrust of government claims about elections, leading to rejection of official narratives that elections are secure.

Very high trust in government and high system justification, combined with low dangerous-worldview and low authoritarian submission, produce mild opposition to distrusting elections, as the person generally trusts institutional processes.
Very low distrust, reflecting extremely high trust in government and system justification, rejecting election skepticism.

Extremely high trust in government (92.18), very high trust in judiciary (90.34) and police (95.51), combined with very high system justification (71.86) and very low punitive attitudes (0.26), produce maximal opposition to distrusting elections, as the profile's core identity is system-affirming and anti-skeptical.

Moderate trust in government (45.16) combined with high collective narcissism (72.71) and low external political efficacy (39.76) produce mild distrust of electoral integrity, as I suspect elites may undermine national sovereignty.
Extremely high trust in government and institutions, combined with high agreeableness and low authoritarianism, leads to strong rejection of election distrust.
